name: Andrographis
summary: Andrographis has moderate human evidence for reducing upper respiratory tract infection symptoms.
description: Andrographis has moderate human evidence for reducing upper respiratory tract infection symptoms.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v8.2: Systematic reviews found Andrographis paniculata superior to placebo for uncomplicated upper respiratory infection symptom relief.
claimMap.notes: v8.2: Systematic reviews found Andrographis paniculata superior to placebo for uncomplicated upper respiratory infection symptom relief.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: andrographis extract dosing varies by product; use standardized andrographolide-labeled products

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 15095142. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 15095142. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Pregnancy (animal and traditional-use evidence of uterine-stimulant/antifertility effects)
autoimmune disease or immunosuppressant therapy without clinician review (andrographis stimulates immune signaling)
anticoagulant/antiplatelet or antihypertensive therapy without monitoring
May potentiate antihypertensive and antidiabetic medications
rare reports of anaphylaxis and elevated liver enzymes at high doses.
